name: Red Cooked Beef with Gravy Noodles
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boned chuck roast or thick steak
2 tbsp Peanut oil or deep frying oil
4 large Stalks Napa cabbage, leaves removed,
recipeInstructions:
RED-COOKING LIQUID
1. Sichuan peppercorns
4 cups Water
1/2 cup Black soy sauce
2 tbsp Chinjiang vinegar
2 Scallions
3 Tablespoons Ginger
1 Piece dried licorice root (optional)
GRAVY INGREDIENTS
1 1/4 cups Chicken stock
2 tbsp Medium sherry
1 tsp Ground bean sauce
1/2 tsp Sugar
Cornstarch paste
Red-Cooking Meat:
1. Toast peppercorns in a hot wok until fragrant; crush and finely mince with a cleaver; reserve.
2. Cut beef into 1-1/2-inch chunks.
3. Heat cooking oil in the hot wok until it starts to smoke; brown beef in several batches; drain oil and discard.
4. Combine peppercorns and remaining ingredients of red-cooking liquid; add to the wok. Bring to boil; reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 1-1/2 to 2 hours, until meat is very tender. (If liquid evaporates before meat is tender, add a cup of water or stock and continue cooking.)
5. Remove meat from the liquid. If desired, cool meat, cover, and refrigerate.
Boiling Noodles:
1. Bring 4 quarts of water to rolling boil; add noodles.
2. Drain when still slightly undercooked.
3. Stir-frying: Heat peanut oil in a hot wok. When oil starts to smoke, add scallions and stir-fry until fragrant.
4. Add garlic, ginger, and Napa cabbage; stir-fry for about 1 minute, until cabbage is slightly wilted.
5. Add meat and all gravy ingredients except cornstarch paste. Reduce heat to medium, cover, and simmer for about 3 minutes.
6. Remove the cover; dribble in enough cornstarch paste to make a light gravy; not too heavy as you want to coat noodles, but not too soupy either.
7. Turn up the heat; when sauce returns to boil, add noodles. Toss to mix (and reheat) noodles. Transfer to warmed serving platter or large bowl.
